Human.bonusLanguages: Any (other than secret languages, such as Druidic)

traits.name: Medium
traits.description: As Medium creatures, humans have no special bonuses or penalties due to their size.

traits.name: Human base land speed
traits.description: Human base land speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Extra feat at 1st level
traits.description: 1 extra feat at 1st level, because humans are quick to master specialized tasks and varied in their talents.

traits.name: Extra skill points
traits.description: 4 extra skill points at 1st level and 1 extra skill point at each additional level, since humans are versatile and capable.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Language: Common. Bonus Languages: Any (other than secret languages, such as Druidic). Humans mingle with all kinds of other folk and thus can learn any language found in an area.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Any. When determining whether a multiclass human takes an experience point penalty, his or her highest-level class does not count.

Dwarf.speedNote: Dwarves can move at this speed even when wearing medium or heavy armor or when carrying a medium or heavy load (unlike other creatures, whose speed is reduced in such situations).

racialAttackBonuses.against: orcs (including half-orcs) and goblinoids (including goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears)

racialACBonuses.against: monsters of the giant type (such as ogres, trolls, and hill giants)

racialSkillBonuses.condition: related to stone or metal items

traits.name: Ability modifiers
traits.description: +2 Constitution, -2 Charisma: Dwarves are stout and tough but tend to be gruff and reserved.

traits.name: Medium
traits.description: Medium: As Medium creatures, dwarves have no special bonuses or penalties due to their size.

traits.name: Dwarf base land speed
traits.description: Dwarf base land speed is 20 feet. However, dwarves can move at this speed even when wearing medium or heavy armor or when carrying a medium or heavy load (unlike other creatures, whose speed is reduced in such situations).

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Darkvision: Dwarves can see in the dark up to 60 feet. Darkvision is black and white only, but it is otherwise like normal sight, and dwarves can function just fine with no light at all.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: Stonecunning: This ability grants a dwarf a +2 racial bonus on Search checks to notice unusual stonework, such as sliding walls, stonework traps, new construction (even when built to match the old), unsafe stone surfaces, shaky stone ceilings, and the like. Something that isn't stone but that is disguised as stone also counts as unusual stonework. A dwarf who merely comes within 10 feet of unusual stonework can make a Search check as if he were actively searching, and a dwarf can use the Search skill to find stonework traps as a rogue can. A dwarf can also intuit depth, sensing his approximate depth underground as naturally as a human can sense which way is up. Dwarves have a sixth sense about stonework, an innate ability that they get plenty of opportunity to practice and hone in their underground homes.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Weapon Familiarity: Dwarves may treat dwarven waraxes and dwarven urgroshes as martial weapons, rather than exotic weapons.

traits.name: Stability
traits.description: Stability: A dwarf gains a +4 bonus on ability checks made to resist being bull rushed or tripped when standing on the ground (but not when climbing, flying, riding, or otherwise not standing firmly on the ground).

traits.name: Saving throws against poison
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on saving throws against poison: Dwarves are hardy and resistant to toxins.

traits.name: Saving throws against spells
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on saving throws against spells and spell-like effects: Dwarves have an innate resistance to magic spells.

traits.name: Attack rolls against orcs and goblinoids
traits.description: +1 racial bonus on attack rolls against orcs (including half-orcs) and goblinoids (including goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears): Dwarves are trained in the special combat techniques that allow them to fight their common enemies more effectively.

traits.name: AC against giants
traits.description: +4 dodge bonus to Armor Class against monsters of the giant type (such as ogres, trolls, and hill giants): This bonus represents special training that dwarves undergo, during which they learn tricks that previous generations developed in their battles with giants. Any time a creature loses its Dexterity bonus (if any) to Armor Class, such as when it's caught flat-footed, it loses its dodge bonus, too.

traits.name: Appraise (stone or metal)
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Appraise checks that are related to stone or metal items: Dwarves are familiar with valuable items of all kinds, especially those made of stone or metal.

traits.name: Craft (stone or metal)
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Craft checks that are related to stone or metal: Dwarves are especially capable with stonework and metalwork.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Dwarven. Bonus Languages: Giant, Gnome, Goblin, Orc, Terran, and Undercommon. Dwarves are familiar with the languages of their enemies and of their subterranean allies.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Fighter. A multiclass dwarf's fighter class does not count when determining whether he takes an experience point penalty for multiclassing. Dwarven culture extols the virtues of battle, and the vocation comes easily to dwarves.

traits.name: Ability modifiers
traits.description: +2 Dexterity, -2 Constitution: Elves are graceful but frail. An elf's grace makes her naturally better at stealth and archery.

traits.name: Medium
traits.description: Medium: As Medium creatures, elves have no special bonuses or penalties due to their size.

traits.name: Elf base land speed
traits.description: Elf base land speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Immunity and saving throw
traits.description: Immunity to magic sleep effects, and a +2 racial saving throw bonus against enchantment spells or effects.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Low-Light Vision: An elf can see twice as far as a human in starlight, moonlight, torchlight, and similar conditions of poor illumination. She retains the ability to distinguish color and detail under these conditions.

traits.name: Weapon Proficiency
traits.description: Weapon Proficiency: Elves receive the Martial Weapon Proficiency feats for the longsword, rapier, longbow (including composite longbow), and shortbow (including composite shortbow) as bonus feats. Elves esteem the arts of swordplay and archery, so all elves are familiar with these weapons.

traits.name: Listen, Search, and Spot
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Listen, Search, and Spot checks. An elf who merely passes within 5 feet of a secret or concealed door is entitled to a Search check to notice it as if she were actively looking for it. An elf's senses are so keen that she practically has a sixth sense about hidden portals.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Elven. Bonus Languages: Draconic, Gnoll, Gnome, Goblin, Orc, and Sylvan. Elves commonly know the languages of their enemies and of their friends, as well as Draconic, the language commonly found in ancient tomes of secret knowledge.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Wizard. A multiclass elf's wizard class does not count when determining whether she takes an experience point penalty for multiclassing. Wizardry comes naturally to elves - indeed, they sometimes claim to have invented it, and fighter/wizards are especially common among them.

racialAttackBonuses.against: kobolds and goblinoids (including goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears)

racialACBonuses.against: monsters of the giant type (such as ogres, trolls, and hill giants)

traits.name: Ability modifiers
traits.description: +2 Constitution, -2 Strength: Like dwarves, gnomes are tough, but they are small and therefore not as strong as larger humanoids.

traits.name: Small
traits.description: Small: As a Small creature, a gnome gains a +1 size bonus to Armor Class, a +1 size bonus on attack rolls, and a +4 size bonus on Hide checks, but he uses smaller weapons than humans use, and his lifting and carrying limits are three-quarters of those of a Medium character.

traits.name: Gnome base land speed
traits.description: Gnome base land speed is 20 feet.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Low-Light Vision: A gnome can see twice as far as a human in starlight, moonlight, torchlight, and similar conditions of poor illumination. He retains the ability to distinguish color and detail under these conditions.

traits.name: Weapon Familiarity
traits.description: Weapon Familiarity: Gnomes may treat gnome hooked hammers as martial weapons rather than exotic weapons.

traits.name: Saving throws against illusions
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on saving throws against illusions: Gnomes are innately familiar with illusions of all kinds.

traits.name: Illusion spell DC
traits.description: Add +1 to the Difficulty Class for all saving throws against illusion spells cast by gnomes. Their innate familiarity with these effects make their illusions more difficult to see through. This adjustment stacks with those from similar effects, such as the Spell Focus feat.

traits.name: Attack rolls against kobolds and goblinoids
traits.description: +1 racial bonus on attack rolls against kobolds and goblinoids (including goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears): Gnomes battle these creatures frequently and practice special techniques for fighting them.

traits.name: AC against giants
traits.description: +4 dodge bonus to Armor Class against monsters of the giant type (such as ogres, trolls, and hill giants): This bonus represents special training that gnomes undergo, during which they learn tricks that previous generations developed in their battles with giants. Any time a creature loses its Dexterity bonus (if any) to Armor Class, such as when it's caught flat-footed, it loses its dodge bonus, too.

traits.name: Listen
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Listen checks: Gnomes have keen ears.

traits.name: Craft (alchemy)
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Craft (alchemy) checks: A gnome's sensitive nose allows him to monitor alchemical processes by smell.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Gnome. Bonus Languages: Draconic, Dwarven, Elven, Giant, Goblin, and Orc. Gnomes deal more with elves and dwarves than elves and dwarves deal with one another, and they learn the languages of their enemies (kobolds, giants, goblins, and orcs) as well. In addition, a gnome can speak with a burrowing mammal (a badger, fox, rabbit, or the like, see below). This ability is innate to gnomes. See the speak with animals spell description.

traits.name: Spell-Like Abilities
traits.description: Spell-Like Abilities: 1/day—speak with animals (burrowing mammal only, duration 1 minute). A gnome with a Charisma score of at least 10 also has the following spell-like abilities: 1/day—dancing lights, ghost sound, prestidigitation. Caster level 1st; save DC 10 + gnome's Cha modifier + spell level.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Bard. A multiclass gnome's bard class does not count when determining whether he takes an experience point penalty.

Half-Elf.bonusLanguages: Any (other than secret languages, such as Druidic)
immunities: Sleep spells and similar magical effects

traits.name: Medium
traits.description: Medium: As Medium creatures, half-elves have no special bonuses or penalties due to their size.

traits.name: Half-elf base land speed
traits.description: Half-elf base land speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Immunity and saving throw
traits.description: Immunity to sleep spells and similar magical effects, and a +2 racial bonus on saving throws against enchantment spells or effects.

traits.name: Low-Light Vision
traits.description: Low-Light Vision: A half-elf can see twice as far as a human in starlight, moonlight, torchlight, and similar conditions of poor illumination. She retains the ability to distinguish color and detail under these conditions.

traits.name: Listen, Search, and Spot
traits.description: +1 racial bonus on Listen, Search, and Spot checks: A half-elf does not have the elf's ability to notice secret doors simply by passing near them. Half-elves have keen senses, but not as keen as those of an elf.

traits.name: Diplomacy and Gather Information
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Diplomacy and Gather Information checks: Half-elves get along naturally with all people.

traits.name: Elven Blood
traits.description: Elven Blood: For all effects related to race, a half-elf is considered an elf. Half-elves, for example, are just as vulnerable to special effects that affect elves as their elf ancestors are, and they can use magic items that are only usable by elves.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Elven. Bonus Languages: Any (other than secret languages, such as Druidic). Half-elves have all the versatility and broad (if shallow) experience that humans have.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Any. When determining whether a multiclass half-elf takes an experience point penalty, her highest-level class does not count.

traits.name: Ability modifiers
traits.description: +2 Strength, -2 Intelligence, -2 Charisma: Half-orcs are strong, but their orc lineage makes them dull and crude. A half-orc's starting Intelligence score is always at least 3. If this adjustment would lower the character's score to 1 or 2, his score is nevertheless 3.

traits.name: Medium
traits.description: Medium: As Medium creatures, half-orcs have no special bonuses or penalties due to their size.

traits.name: Half-orc base land speed
traits.description: Half-orc base land speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Darkvision: Half-orcs (and orcs) can see in the dark up to 60 feet. Darkvision is black and white only, but it is otherwise like normal sight, and half-orcs can function just fine with no light at all.

traits.name: Orc Blood
traits.description: Orc Blood: For all effects related to race, a half-orc is considered an orc. Half-orcs, for example, are just as vulnerable to special effects that affect orcs as their orc ancestors are, and they can use magic items that are only usable by orcs.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Orc. Bonus Languages: Draconic, Giant, Gnoll, Goblin, and Abyssal. Smart half-orcs (who are rare) may know the languages of their allies or rivals.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Barbarian. A multiclass half-orc's barbarian class does not count when determining whether he takes an experience point penalty. Ferocity runs in a half-orc's veins.

traits.name: Ability modifiers
traits.description: +2 Dexterity, -2 Strength: Halflings are quick, agile, and good with ranged weapons, but they are small and therefore not as strong as other humanoids.

traits.name: Small
traits.description: Small: As a Small creature, a halfling gains a +1 size bonus to Armor Class, a +1 size bonus on attack rolls, and a +4 size bonus on Hide checks, but she uses smaller weapons than humans use, and her lifting and carrying limits are three-quarters of those of a Medium character.

traits.name: Halfling base land speed
traits.description: Halfling base land speed is 20 feet.

traits.name: Climb, Jump, and Move Silently
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Climb, Jump, and Move Silently checks: Halflings are agile, surefooted, and athletic.

traits.name: Saving throws
traits.description: +1 racial bonus on all saving throws: Halflings are surprisingly capable of avoiding mishaps.

traits.name: Saving throws against fear
traits.description: +2 morale bonus on saving throws against fear. This bonus stacks with the halfling's +1 bonus on saving throws in general.

traits.name: Thrown weapons and slings
traits.description: +1 racial bonus on attack rolls with thrown weapons and slings: Throwing and slinging stones is a universal sport among halflings, and they develop especially good aim.

traits.name: Listen
traits.description: +2 racial bonus on Listen checks: Halflings have keen ears.

traits.name: Automatic and bonus languages
traits.description: Automatic Languages: Common and Halfling. Bonus Languages: Dwarven, Elven, Gnome, Goblin, and Orc. Smart halflings learn the languages of their friends and enemies.

traits.name: Favored Class
traits.description: Favored Class: Rogue. A multiclass halfling's rogue class does not count when determining whether she takes an experience point penalty for multiclassing. Halflings have long had to rely on stealth, wit, and skill, and the vocation of rogue comes naturally to them.
